name: GraphQL error responses leak schema field suggestions
description: When a query references a non-existent field, most GraphQL servers respond with 'Did you mean "<closest>"?' — a friendly debugging aid for developers, but a schema-enumeration vector for anyone with network access. The suggestions reveal real field names without requiring introspection. Useful as a backup-channel attack after introspection has been disabled.

vulnerability.remediation: Disable field-suggestions in production. HotChocolate: set `IRequestExecutorBuilder.ModifyRequestOptions(o => o.IncludeExceptionDetails = false)` and configure a custom IErrorFilter that strips 'Did you mean' messages. Apollo: `formatError: err => { if (/Did you mean/.test(err.message)) return new Error('Bad Request'); return err; }`. Strawberry: same pattern via the error formatter.
